skill.name: hig-patterns
skill.description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines reference skill for interaction patterns, including onboarding, loading, drag and drop, search, settings, notifications, modality, media, accounts, and feedback.
skill.summary: Apply Apple HIG interaction patterns to app flows, states, and platform-specific UX decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found they come from Apple HIG Markdown references, image URLs, and inline API names. No executable network calls, shell execution, credential access, malicious intent, or prompt injection were found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The reported URL findings are Apple Developer source links, documentation links, image assets, and publisher links in Markdown. They do not execute requests or send user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown source, image, or reference links to Apple and publisher pages. There is no code path that performs network I/O or exfiltrates data.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are inline Markdown formatting around Apple API names and local context path text. They are not shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax appears inside prose files and wraps identifiers such as ShareLink and a context file path. No executable script, interpreter invocation, or command substitution exists.

low_findings.title: Credential, Crypto, And C2 Heuristics Are Documentation Terms
low_findings.description: The sensitive, weak cryptography, and C2 keyword detections map to HIG guidance about passkeys, Keychain, tokens, media playback links, and CDN asset hashes. These are educational references, not credential access or malicious infrastructure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is Apple design guidance and video/image references. I found no code that reads secrets, implements cryptography, or contacts command-and-control endpoints.

content.value_statement: Apple app flows often fail when interaction patterns are chosen without platform guidance. This skill maps UX questions to practical HIG patterns and tradeoffs.
actual_capabilities: Selects HIG-aligned patterns for onboarding, loading, search, settings, notifications, and modality.
Explains feedback choices across visual, audio, haptic, and progress states.
Provides platform considerations for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS.
References focused Markdown files for each interaction pattern and UX scenario.
Identifies common HIG pitfalls such as premature sign-in, excessive modals, and missing undo support.
Connects account, sharing, file, media, workout, and data-entry flows to Apple design guidance.
limitations: It provides design guidance, not production-ready Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit, or AppKit implementation code.
It depends on included Markdown references and may not reflect future Apple documentation changes.
It does not replace accessibility testing, platform QA, or App Review policy analysis.
It cannot inspect an app unless the user provides screens, flows, or product requirements.

use_cases.title: Design A New Mobile Flow
use_cases.description: Choose HIG-aligned onboarding, permissions, loading, and empty-state patterns before creating screens.

use_cases.title: Review App Interaction Quality
use_cases.description: Audit existing app flows for modal overuse, unclear feedback, missing undo, and platform mismatch.

use_cases.title: Plan Platform-Specific Behavior
use_cases.description: Adapt search, drag and drop, media playback, and multitasking behavior across Apple platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Pick A Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one UX decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hig-patterns to recommend the best Apple HIG pattern for this screen: [describe screen, goal, platform, and user action].

prompt_templates.title: Improve A Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a multi-step experience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hig-patterns to review this app flow for HIG issues: [paste steps]. Focus on modality, feedback, loading, permissions, and undo.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Platform Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hig-patterns to compare how this feature should behave on iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS: [feature].

prompt_templates.title: Create A Design Specification
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced UX specification for implementation handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hig-patterns to produce a HIG-aligned interaction specification for [feature]. Include states, feedback, errors, accessibility concerns, and pitfalls.

output_examples.input: Should my productivity app require sign-in during onboarding?
output_examples.output: Recommendation: defer sign-in unless the core task requires an account. Let people explore first, then explain account benefits at the moment they need syncing or collaboration.

output_examples.input: How should I handle a destructive delete action in an iPad app?
output_examples.output: Recommendation: prefer undo when the action can be reversed. Use confirmation only when data loss is permanent, sensitive, or legally significant.

output_examples.input: My video app has a custom player on visionOS. What should I check?
output_examples.output: Review whether the system player already meets the need. Preserve expected playback controls, avoid automatic immersion, and keep people comfortable during viewing.

best_practices: Provide the platform, user goal, and surrounding flow before asking for a pattern recommendation.
Ask for rationale and pitfalls so the output can guide design reviews and implementation handoff.
Pair this skill with accessibility, privacy, and component-specific HIG guidance for final decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for current App Review rules or legal compliance review.
Do not ask for generic UX advice without naming the Apple platform and interaction context.
Do not apply one platform pattern blindly across every Apple device family.

faq.question: What does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Apple HIG interaction patterns for app flows, states, feedback, onboarding, loading, search, settings, media, accounts, and related UX decisions.

faq.question: Can it write SwiftUI code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation choices, but it is primarily a design reference skill, not a code generation skill.

faq.question: Which platforms are included?
faq.answer: The references include guidance for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS when Apple provides platform considerations.

faq.question: Does it replace Apple documentation?
faq.answer: No. It organizes included HIG references for AI-assisted guidance, but final decisions should check current Apple documentation when accuracy is critical.

faq.question: Why did security analysis flag many issues?
faq.answer: The scanner matched URLs, inline code formatting, Keychain terms, and asset hashes inside Markdown documentation. Manual review found no executable threat.

faq.question: How should I get the best answer?
faq.answer: Describe the user goal, current flow, platform, sensitive actions, and any constraints such as offline use or accessibility requirements.
